revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:吾妻神社 (御殿場市)]]
== Infobox == {{infobox religious building | name = Azuma Shrine (Gotemba) | image = {{ill|image:Atago-shrine Isesaki 001.JPG|ja|愛宕神社（御殿場市）|lt=Atago Shrine (Gotemba)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/image:Atago-shrine Isesaki 001.JPG|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}} [[:en:Gotemba City|Gotemba]], Yubaru 196 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.315972|N|138.940389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|lt=Yamato Takeru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}; also enshrined is Tōshō Daikongen (representing {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}) | type = | creation = 1616 (Genwa 2) | festival = Early April | location = {{ill|Gotemba|qid=Q735175|1=Gotemba, Shizuoka}} }} == Overview == '''Azuma Shrine (Gotemba)''' (吾妻神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Gotemba, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It stands on the site of the former palace of Tokugawa Ieyasu and also enshrines Tōshō Daikongen, reflecting its dual function as a traditional shrine and a fire-prevention memorial. == Additional Information == It is closely associated with local pilgrimage and is part of the regional fire-protection network. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.akihasan.or.jp/ {{ill|Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine|en|Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine|qid=Q500413|lt=Akihayama Honmiya Akiha Shrine|1=Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine}}] (Official site)
{{translated page|ja|吾妻神社 (御殿場市)|version=100827166|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:吾妻神社 (御殿場市)]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
